agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider 2025 Annual Municipal Report and audited financial statements
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and its accompanying Highlights document. This report includes the city's audited financial statements for the past year. While this is a routine presentation, it gives business owners a look at the city's financial health and spending priorities. Council will vote on whether to accept the report at a future meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider 2025 Annual Municipal Report and Highlights
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and a separate Highlights document at an upcoming meeting. This report summarizes the city's activities, finances, and achievements for the past year. It is not yet approved — council will review and decide whether to accept it. Business owners should watch for the council decision, as the report may contain information about city services, fees, or programs that affect local businesses.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to replace Development Procedures Bylaw, effective July 1, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on May 12, 2026 to repeal the Development Procedures Bylaw (2022) and replace it with the Development Procedures Bylaw (2026), effective July 1, 2026. The new bylaw consolidates all development application procedures, introduces new development permit types, and updates fees in the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. Related amendments to the Building Bylaw also take effect July 1, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on May 12, 2026 — if approved, new fees and procedures apply to applications submitted on or after July 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Council vote on new Development Procedures Bylaw
New Development Procedures Bylaw takes effect

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 and fee changes effective July 1, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council approved repealing the Development Procedures Bylaw 2022 and replacing it with the Development Procedures Bylaw 2026, effective July 1, 2026. The new bylaw consolidates all development application procedures, introduces new development permit types, and updates fees in the Building Bylaw and Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. Transition rules apply for applications in progress.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review the new fee schedule and procedural requirements before submitting any development or building permit application after July 1, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to replace Development Procedures Bylaw, update building fees, effective July 1, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing the current Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw (2022) and replacing it with a new Development Procedures Bylaw (2026), effective July 1, 2026. The proposal also includes amendments to the Burnaby Building Bylaw and the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, introducing new development permit types, expanded delegated authorities, and formalized fee and refund practices. If approved, these changes would update the rules and fees for development applications, building permits, and related processes in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on May 12, 2026 — if approved, new application rules and fees take effect July 1, 2026, affecting your development or building project costs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on DAP program update and award application
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing the final report on Phase 3 of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. This program aims to make the city's development approval system faster and more efficient. If endorsed, the city would also apply for a 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Award, which recognizes innovative local government projects. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.

Purpose: To provide an update on the activities and outcomes of the
Development Approvals Process (DAP) program and seek endorsement
to apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to apply for UBCM award on development approvals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing an application for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards. This is based on the final report for Phase 3 of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. The DAP program aims to make the process of getting development permits faster and simpler. If endorsed, the application would recognize Burnaby's work on improving how development applications are reviewed and approved. This is a proposed step — no decision has been made yet.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on $1.1M park aeration contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding a $1.1 million contract for aeration and overseeding services for city parks and sports fields. Aeration is the process of poking small holes in the soil to let air, water, and nutrients reach the grass roots. Overseeding means spreading new grass seed over existing turf to thicken the lawn. If approved, this contract would pay a company to perform this work on public green spaces across Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.

agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for aeration and overseeding services. If approved, this would authorize the city to hire a company to aerate (poke small holes in the soil to let air, water, and nutrients reach the roots) and overseed (spread new grass seed over existing lawns) on city-owned land. This is a routine maintenance contract and does not create new rules or fees for private businesses. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider comment letters on Metro 2050 land-use amendments
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider sending comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District about two proposed changes to the Metro 2050 regional growth strategy. One amendment is proposed by the City of Maple Ridge (for the North 256 Street area) and the other by the City of Surrey (for the Hazelmere area). These are site-specific amendments that would change how land in those areas can be used under the regional plan. Council has not yet decided what to say in the letters — this item is up for discussion and a vote at the upcoming meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on removing office space for 292 more homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the approved plan for a large development at 3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place. The original plan, already approved in 2024, allowed for two residential towers (80 and 73 storeys) with a 13-storey base that included retail and office space. The new proposal would remove the commercial office use and replace it with 292 additional market strata residential units (condos you can buy). This means more homes but no new office space in this project. The bylaw has already been replaced by a newer version (Bylaw #14808) that got its second reading on April 28, 2026, so the final decision is still pending.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the final council vote on Bylaw #14808 — this could affect nearby property values and development plans.

key_dates.label: Second reading of Bylaw #14808 (replacement bylaw) — Burnaby
